title: Junior IT-Projekt-Consultant / Digitale Preisauszeichnung (ESL) (m/w/d)
required_skills: Interest in retail hardware / ESL (Electronic Shelf Labels) / network and software components
bonus_skills: Willingness to travel for site visits
matched_skills: Agile sprint planning and delivery (Scrum) — demonstrated at Hapag-Lloyd and HSBC
Fluent German and English — meets bilingual international team requirement
Cross-functional stakeholder coordination and communication of complex topics
Managing multiple workstreams simultaneously (PMO operations at HSBC, project coordination at Hapag-Lloyd)
Collaborative mindset in international team environments
missing_skills: ESL / Electronic Shelf Labels domain knowledge
Retail hardware, network, or software infrastructure experience
Interest or background in physical retail digitalisation
Willingness / practicality to relocate or commute to Weinsberg (~550km from Lüneburg)
core_responsibilities: Manage agile implementation of digital price labeling (ESL) solutions and oversee software development sprints
Coordinate software updates and system changes to maintain high system availability
Communicate complex technical issues clearly to stakeholders and international teams
Adapt to changing project requirements and manage multiple workstreams simultaneously
Conduct occasional site visits to retail locations to support practical roll-out of ESL solutions
cv_highlights: Agile sprint ceremonies (sprint planning, retrospectives) at Hapag-Lloyd — directly relevant to overseeing development sprints
PMO operations and technology operations at HSBC — demonstrates IT project governance experience
Stakeholder communication and C-level reporting — transferable to communicating technical issues to diverse audiences
Fluent German + English satisfies the explicit bilingual requirement for international team communication
Cross-functional coordination across multiple workstreams aligns with multi-project management expectation
gaps: No ESL or retail hardware/digitalisation domain experience — this is a core focus of the role
No background in retail price labeling, POS systems, or related retail tech
Location gap: Weinsberg is ~550km from Lüneburg; relocation or heavy travel required
Role is 'Junior' level which may underutilise Boru's 4+ years of international PMO/coordination experience
recommended_emphasis: Emphasise agile sprint management and delivery experience (Hapag-Lloyd) as direct match for overseeing software development sprints
Highlight ability to communicate complex technical topics to non-technical and international stakeholders
Stress bilingual (German/English) professional proficiency explicitly — it is a stated requirement
Frame PMO and technology operations experience at HSBC as evidence of IT project lifecycle management
Acknowledge and frame adaptability to new technical domains as a strength (e.g., quickly ramping up on ESL/retail tech)
recommendation: Borderline — apply with tailoring. Agile project management and bilingual skills are strong matches. However, the ESL/retail hardware domain is a significant gap, and the 550km location distance is a practical concern. Only pursue if open to relocation or if the company explicitly supports remote/hybrid for this role. The 'Junior' label may also signal a lower seniority target than Boru's profile.
